rulings.Case Title: CESAR ASCARRUNZ VS. HOWARD HSU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT HOWARD HSU'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Granted as to the motion to set aside default, and denied as to the motion to quash service of summons. Default is set aside because Plaintiff failed to comply with the requirement of CCP § 587 when he mailed the request for entry of default to a incorrect address for Defendant Hsu. (See Hsu Declaration in support of motion, Ex. B.) Plaintiff concedes that both the name of the street and zip code are incorrect. Although titled a motion to quash, Defendant Hsu in essence moves to dismiss pursuant to CCP § 583.250. The Court finds that the filing of the Second Amended Complaint alleges additional different facts than the Complaint, and the service of summons runs from the date of amendment. (Barrington v. A. H. Robins Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 146, 154.) Now that the default has been set aside, and Defendant Hsu admits he has been properly served, Defendant Hsu may respond to the Second Amended Complaint in compliance with the timing requirements in the Code of Civil Procedure. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL GRAGNANI VS. CLARENDON NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion For Leave To File Amended Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANT CLARENDON NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Leave To File Amended Cross Complaint The motion is granted. The proposed cross-complaint is compulsory and Plaintiff fails to present substantial evidence demonstrating bad faith. See Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank, (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99. The statute of limitations does not bar the amended cross-complaint and the prejudice to Plaintiff from the filing of the cross-complaint is minimal. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: BEVERLY BERNELL MYRES VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Joyce Armstrong'S Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, July 31, 2013, Line 1: Defendant SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S Motion to Compel Joyce Armstrong's Deposition Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion. No costs or fees are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JUVENTINO ESTRADA VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Comepl Further Deposition Of Pltf; Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, July 31, 2013, Line 3: DEFENDANT NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Comepl Further Deposition Of Plaintiff; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court is not satisfied that the parties have fully met their obligation to meet and confer about the specific issues raised by the motion. Counsel for the parties are directed to confer prior to the hearing (perhaps at 8:00 AM on the day of the hearing) and attempt to simplify and condense the matters on which they are not in agreement. Counsel should be prepared to present the court with the results of their further negotiations. A ruling shall be deferred until this additional meet and confer requirement has been fulfilled. =(302/JPT)

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court is not satisfied that the parties have fully met their obligation to meet and confer regarding the discovery issues in dispute. Counsel for the parties are directed to meet and confer prior to the hearing (perhaps at 8:00 AM on the morning of the hearing) and make a good faith attempt to simplify and condense the matters on which they are not in agreement. Counsel shall be prepared to present the court with the results of these negotiations at the time set for the hearing on the merits. A ruling shall be deferred until this additional meet and confer requirement has been met. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GESTAMP ASETYM SOLAR NORTH AMERICA, INC. VS. GCL SOLAR SOLUTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT GCL SOLAR SOLUTIONS, INC., GCL SOLAR ENERGY, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Denied. Whether the exculpatory provision (Terms and Conditions, 19) applies to limit the damages sought is a question of fact that is not proper subject of a motion to strike. The pleadings are not irrelevant, false or improper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: GESTAMP ASETYM SOLAR NORTH AMERICA, INC. VS. GCL SOLAR SOLUTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT GCL SOLAR SOLUTIONS, INC., GCL SOLAR ENERGY, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege each of the four causes of action. Whether the exculpatory provision (Terms and Conditions, 19) applies is a question of fact that is not proper subject of a demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
